body: SLS(30)/9.44 compiled clean: 0 errors, 1 pre-existing warning (sesMap, same as SLS24 build, not introduced here). Real .ex4 (2,618,010 bytes) attached alongside the compile log. Not yet attached to a chart or backtested -- WARRANT#9's predicted effect (regimes r3/r4/r8 at n=387/140/150 should now retain prior instead of computing a doomed-to-overfit IC) still needs a real calibration run to confirm. Recommend attaching it to the USDJPY M5 chart the same way as SLS(29) rather than me touching MT4's chart-template file directly -- I did not launch terminal.exe this time to avoid any repeat collision, and editing the binary .chr template programmatically carries real corruption risk I'd rather not take without cause.
